data.title: Sofia share indices keep rising, Chimimport tops blue-chip gainers
data.description:
SOFIA (Bulgaria), January 10 (SeeNews) - All five indices of the Bulgarian Stock Exchange (BSE) closed in green territory on Friday, with the blue-chip SOFIX edging up 0.03% to 893.13 points, bourse data showed.

SOFIX was buoyed by a 2.86% climb in diversified group Chimimport [BUL:CHIM] to 0.72 levs and a 1.03% rise in diversified group Doverie United Holding [BUL:DUH] to 7.82 levs.

Chimimport and Doverie further lifted the BGTR30 index, in which companies with a free float of at least 10% have equal weight. It advanced by 0.12% to 863.10 points, additionally helped by a 3.00% increase in software and IT services group Wiser Technology [BUL:WISR] to 5.15 levs.

The BGBX40, which tracks the 40 most traded shares on the Sofia bourse, added 0.07% to 172.34 points. The index was boosted by the gains in Chimimport and Wiser, along with a 3.67% growth in pawnshop chain operator Sofia Commerce-Pawn Brokerage [BUL:SCOM] to 5.65 levs.

The BGREIT index, which tracks seven real estate investment trusts, ended 0.11% higher at 214.99 points, driven by a 0.86% uptick in Balkan and Sea Properties REIT [BUL:BSP] to 23.40 levs.

A 5.20% jump in electric vehicle manufacturer Sin Cars Industry [BUL:SIN] to 0.364 levs pushed up the BEAMX, which tracks the ten most highly capitalised growth companies, by 0.58% to 100.50 points.

Total turnover on the main market of the Sofia bourse went up to 337,314 levs ($177,708/172,465 euro) on Friday from 255,759 levs on Thursday.

Farming holding Agria Group [BUL:AGH] generated the highest turnover of the day, which came in at 63,871 levs after 3,320 shares traded. The stock closed 1.03% higher at 19.60 levs.

(1 euro = 1.95583 levs)

data.title: Delta Air Lines Reports Lower Jet Fuel Costs in 4Q, Full Year 2024 — OPIS
data.description:
Delta Air Lines released fourth-quarter and full-year 2024 financial results Friday morning, showing lower jet fuel costs and slightly higher use.

For the final quarter of 2024, Delta spent $2.409 billion on fuel and related costs, an 18% decline from the $2.941 billion spent in the same quarter a year prior.

Despite spending less year-on-year, the airline used less fuel. In the fourth quarter of 2024, Delta reported using 1.021 billion gallons of jet fuel, which is 4% lower than the 978 million gallons used in the last quarter of 2023.

Likewise, the price of fuel per gallon was significantly lower year-on-year. In the last three months of 2024, Delta paid on average $2.36/gal for fuel, a 21% decline from the $3.01/gal seen in the fourth quarter of 2023. In 2024, Delta, which operates the Trainer, Pa., refinery to help offset fuel costs and provide jet fuel to the airline, reported a 4cts/gal additional cost from the refinery impact.

Looking at fuel cost per available seat mile (ASM), of the cost of fuel extrapolated to each individual seat, figures were much lower in 2024 than 2023. The fuel cost per ASM in the fourth quarter of 2024 was 3.34cts/gal, almost a full penny lower and some 22.35% less than the 4.30cts/ASM fuel cost seen in the fourth quarter of 2023.

Delta also released its full year 2024 financial results as well.

In 2024, Delta spent $20.566 billion on fuel, which is 5% lower than 2023 when the air carrier reported a fuel cost of $11.069 billion.

Like with the fourth quarter, full-year 2024 fuel use was some 5% higher year-on-year. In 2024, Delta reported using 4.114 billion gallons, as opposed to the 3.926 billion gallons in 2023.

Likewise, the cost per fuel was down--albeit not as much as for the fourth quarter. In 2024, the airline paid $2.57/gal for fuel on average, 9% lower than the $2.82/gal for fuel paid on average through 2023.

In terms of cost per available seat mile for fuel, 2024 saw figures drop 10%, from 4.07cts/ASM in 2023 to 3.66cts/ASM in 2024.

Delta Air Lines also highlighted plans with Minnesota SAF (Sustainable Aviation Fuel) Hub to open a new blending facility in 2025, and in 2024 successfully transported SAF via pipeline to Minneapolis-Saint Paul International Airport and Detroit Metropolitan Wayne County Airport.

This content was created by Oil Price Information Service, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. OPIS is run independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

  • Reporting by Cory Wilchek, cwilchek@opisnet.com; Editing by Jason Titze, jtitze@opisnet.com

data.title: GMTech Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
GMTech Inc., a Wyoming-based IT consulting firm specializing in CRM systems, corporate websites, and mobile app development, has released its annual Form 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the competitive IT consulting industry.

Financial Highlights

Revenue, net: $52,800, a decrease from $86,199 in the previous year due to a reduction in IT services engagement with new clients.

Gross Profit: $43,300, reflecting a decrease from $62,699 in the prior year.

Net income (loss): $(32,557), compared to a net income of $2,373 in the previous year, primarily due to increased general and administrative expenses.

Net income (loss) per share-Basic and diluted: 0.00, consistent with the previous year.

Business Highlights

Business Overview: GMTech Inc., through its wholly owned subsidiary, offers IT consulting services and development solutions for CRM systems, corporate websites, and mobile phone apps.

AI Development Tool: The company is planning to develop an AI-powered development tool to automate the design, development, and maintenance of CRM systems, websites, and apps, aiming to reduce the need for external engineers and consultants.

Principal Products and Services: GMTech provides customized CRM system development, website development solutions including e-commerce and web applications, and mobile application development for iOS and Android platforms.

Office Location: The business office is located at 45 Rockefeller Plaza, New York, New York.

Employees: The company currently has two employees, including its officer and director, Yuyang Cui, and director Jianting Liu.

Marketing Strategy: GMTech focuses on online marketing, utilizing social media platforms like Facebook, Twitter, Instagram, and YouTube to promote its services. The company also plans to leverage its President's personal networks and industry associations.

Competition: The IT consulting industry is highly competitive with low barriers to entry. GMTech faces competition from both large high-tech companies and smaller firms.

Government Regulation: The company must comply with applicable regulations but does not anticipate these will materially impact its business operations.

Future Outlook: GMTech plans to enhance its service offerings with the development of the AI Development Tool, which is expected to improve efficiency and reduce reliance on external resources.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Acquisitions: GMTech, Inc. has focused on expanding its ownership and operational capabilities through strategic acquisitions. On October 1, 2024, the company acquired 100% ownership of Fengyi Global Co., LTD., and on November 12, 2024, it acquired Shenggang Excellence Limited. These acquisitions are part of the company's strategy to enhance its market presence and operational scope.

Capital Management: The company raised $140,000 through the issuance of 7,000,000 shares of common stock at $0.02 per share during February and March 2024. This capital was primarily used to support the company's operational activities and strategic acquisitions. As of October 31, 2024, GMTech, Inc. had 12,000,000 shares of common stock issued and outstanding. The company does not have any equity compensation or stock option plans and has not declared or paid any dividends, opting instead to reinvest earnings into business operations.

Future Outlook: GMTech, Inc. plans to retain all available funds and any future earnings for operational use, indicating a focus on reinvestment to support growth and expansion. The company does not anticipate paying cash dividends in the foreseeable future, suggesting a strategy centered on internal development and strategic acquisitions to drive long-term growth.

Challenges and Risks

Industry Competition: The IT consulting industry in which GMTech Inc. operates is highly competitive and fragmented, with limited barriers to entry. This results in frequent new competitors entering the market, many of whom possess substantially greater resources. The company faces significant competition for potential clients and technical personnel from both large high-tech companies and smaller workshops.

Financial Stability: The company experienced a reduction in IT services engagement with new clients, leading to a decrease in revenue from $86,199 in 2023 to $52,800 in 2024. This reduction poses a risk to the company's financial stability and growth prospects. Additionally, the increase in general and administrative expenses due to audit fees and public filing requirements has impacted the company's net income, resulting in a net loss of $32,557 in 2024 compared to a net income of $2,373 in 2023.

Liquidity and Financing: The company’s liquidity improved with cash increasing to $107,534 in 2024 from $22,099 in 2023, primarily due to proceeds from an initial public offering. However, the reliance on financing activities for cash flow presents a risk if future financing is not available or is more costly.

SEC Filing: GMTech Inc. [ GMTH ] - 10-K - Jan. 10, 2025

data.title: Stenocare Secures DKK2 Million Rights Issue Guarantee From Exelity
data.description:
Exelity committed to subscribe for 2 million Danish kroner of Stenocare's ongoing conditional rights issue worth up to 20.2 million kroner.

Exelity's guarantee, along with the previous binding pre-subscription pledges and compensation-free commitments from Stenocare's management and existing shareholders, allowed the Danish medical cannabis group to secure 17.3% of the total rights issue and 38.5% of the required minimum subscription value, according to a Friday release.

Stenocare in December 2024 announced its offer of up to 40,424,994 new shares at 0.50 krone apiece to ensure sufficient capital and to secure its cash runway for 2025 and 2026. The subscription period closes on Jan. 20, 2025.

data.title: Symrise Gains Swedish Regulatory Clearance for Proposed Takeover of Probi
data.description:
Symrise secured all necessary regulatory and authority approvals for its cash takeover offer of Probi following clearance from the Swedish Inspectorate of Strategic Products.

The offer will close Jan. 15 but may be extended, according to a Friday filing.

In November 2024, the German chemicals company announced its takeover offer for the probiotic product developer. The Probi independent bid committee unanimously recommended the bid.

As of Nov. 26, 2024, Symrise held a 90.2% stake in Probi.

data.title: Nvidia's stock is down 9% from its recent record. That shows the short term is winning out for now.
data.description:
By Emily Bary

Nvidia's stock is off 9% from an all-time high hit earlier this week, with investors focused more on the here and now than on new eye-popping projections of market opportunity

Nvidia Corp.'s stock is falling toward its worst weekly performance since September, highlighting that the short term is winning out for now when it comes to investor perceptions.

The stock's 3.2% drop Friday comes as technology stocks in general are under pressure in light of a strong jobs report that has investors more jittery about inflation.

The decline builds on those seen earlier this week in the wake of Chief Executive Jensen Huang's keynote at the CES tech conference, a speech that was big on long-term version but perhaps lacking in terms of the sort of granular detail found on quarterly financials or information about the company's data-center roadmap that investors may have desired, especially in light of a run-up in Nvidia shares (NVDA) to start the year.

See more: Nvidia's stock suffers worst drop in months as CES speech lacked one big thing

Meanwhile, HSBC analyst Frank Lee wrote of a "supply-chain overhang" for Nvidia's new Blackwell products, which could limit the company's beat-and-raise potential in the immediate term.

The stock is off 6.2% so far this week, which would translate to its worst weekly decline since a drop of 13.9% seen the week that ended Sept. 6, 2024, according to Dow Jones Market Data. Shares are down more than 9% from an all-time closing high achieved Monday.

Read: Nvidia's stock falls further - but analysts see a future that's brighter than ever

The selloff in Nvidia shares shows that Wall Street is more focused on the here and now than on the eye-popping futuristic numbers that Nvidia outlined at CES.

For instance, the company has an "almost incomprehensibly large" total addressable market, according to Melius Research analyst Ben Reitzes, when you take into account Nvidia's latest commentary on the opportunities in autonomous driving and robotics. Each of those markets could be worth multiple trillions of dollars, in Huang's view.

"The best total addressable markets are those that are open-ended," Reitzes wrote.

Don't miss: Quantum-computing stocks sink as Nvidia CEO delivers a reality check

-Emily Bary

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Modelo Remains Top Beer Brand in U.S., Constellation Brands CEO Says — Market Talk
data.description: Modelo Especial grew depletions by over 3% in its fiscal third quarter and upheld its position as the top share gainer in U.S. track channels, Constellation Brands' CEO Bill Newlands says on a call with analysts. Corona Extra depletions, on the other hand, declined about 1%, but continued to increase its dollar share as a top ten gainer in the category, Newlands says. "We remain positive about the outlook for Corona Extras growth, as it continues to be the most beloved beer brand in the U.S., and has responded well to the incremental marketing spend deployed in the third quarter," Newlands says. Pacifico delivered another quarter of "very strong" depletion growth of nearly 20%, the executive adds. (sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix)

data.title: Bitcoin may fall 20% to $73,000 if it breaks below this key support level
data.description:
By Frances Yue

This chart shows bitcoin is forming a top based on the so-called shoulder-head-shoulder pattern

Bitcoin may face a downside potential to $73,000 if it breaks below its support level at around $90,680, according to Kim Kramer Larsson, an analyst at Saxo Bank.

The largest cryptocurrency by market capitalization (BTCUSD) has fallen 3.9% over the past five days while it is up 2.6% on Friday to trade at around $94,061, according to the Dow Jones Market Data. The crypto is roughly 13.2% away from its record high at $108,309, reached on Dec. 17.

Bitcoin is forming a top based on the so-called shoulder-head-shoulder pattern, a chart formation in technical analysis that predicts a bullish-to-bearish trend reversal, Kramer Larsson wrote in a Friday post on X.

A head-and-shoulders pattern usually consists of three peaks, with the outer two close in height and the middle one being the highest.

If bitcoin breaks below its neckline support at around $90,680, it could potentially fall to $73,000 next, noted Kramer Larsson.

Some traders have been taking profits as bitcoin rose 120% in 2024.

Read: Higher bond yields could knock bitcoin back down to $90,000

-Frances Yue

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Tilray Reports Q2 Revenue Growth, but Stock Drops Over 11% on Continued Losses
data.description:
With a 9% year-over-year rise in net sales to $211 million, Tilray Brands (TLRY, Financials) released financial results for the second quarter of fiscal 2025. Investors responded to ongoing net losses and inconsistent sector performance, thus the company's shares dropped 11.3% on Friday, ending at $1.22, down $0.15.

Revenue climbed 10% adjusted for constant currency; gross profit jumped 29% year over year to $61 million. Rising from 24% in the previous year, gross margins now show 29%. With $75 million ascribed to non-cash chargesincluding foreign currency losses and stock-based compensationTilray reported a net loss of $85 million for the quarter. At $2 million, adjusted net loss matched last year. Reflecting expenses related to reducing stock-keeping units in the beverage division, adjusted profits before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization came in at $9 million, somewhat down from $10 million a year earlier.

Supported by better gross margins of 40%, up from 34% in the previous year, revenue in the beverage alcohol category jumped 36% to $63 million. While the wellness section saw a 13% rise in income to $15 million, cannabis revenue was constant at $66 million. Slightly over $67 million in the previous year, the distribution division made $68 million.

Targeting $25 million in operational synergies, the business kept carrying out its Project 420 effort. Through operational streamlining and cost cuts at the end of the quarter, Tilray had saved $17 million. Still, these reductions have not completely offset expenditures in infrastructure and marketing, which influences cash flow and changed EBITDA for the quarter.

Emphasizing its emphasis on increasing its footprint in the beverage, cannabis, and wellness areas, Tilray confirmed its fiscal 2025 revenue projection of $950 million to $1 billion. Through continuous operational simplification and strategic investments, the firm hopes to be profitable and expand over long term, according to its statement.

data.title: Cocoa Futures at Near 2-Week Low
data.description:
Cocoa futures traded at around $10,650 per tonne, their lowest level since late December, due to long liquidation and the strength of the dollar.

Meanwhile, traders continued to monitor the supply outlook in key producing region of West Africa.

Dealers reported that while deliveries in Ghana have been well above last season's levels, they are now slowing, and a decline in port arrivals in the Ivory Coast is expected to begin next month.

Additionally, signs of shrinking global cocoa stockpiles persisted.

ICE-monitored cocoa inventories held in US ports have been trending lower for the past 1-1/2 years and fell to a 20-year low of 1,317,220 bags as of January 9th.

Global cocoa production is estimated to have declined by 14% in the 2023-24 season, falling to 4.2 million metric tons (mmt) from 4.9 mmt in 2022-23.

Cocoa prices nearly tripled over 2024, driven by concerns over poor weather conditions in West Africa, compounded by strong seasonal demand
